canonical_name: Dr. YSR Aarogyasri Health Care Scheme (Andhra Pradesh)

local_names.hi: डॉ. वाईएसआर आरोग्यश्री स्वास्थ्य योजना (आंध्र प्रदेश)
local_names.te: డాక్టర్ వైఎస్ఆర్ ఆరోగ్యశ్రీ ఆరోగ్య సంరక్షణ పథకం (ఆంధ్రప్రదేశ్)
local_names.mr: डॉ. वायएसआर आरोग्यश्री आरोग्य योजना (आंध्र प्रदेश)
local_names.gu: ડૉ. વાયએસઆર આરોગ્યશ્રી આરોગ્ય યોજના (આંધ્ર પ્રદેશ)
local_names.pa: ਡਾ. ਵਾਈਐੱਸਆਰ ਆਰੋਗਯਸ਼੍ਰੀ ਸਿਹਤ ਯੋਜਨਾ (ਆਂਧਰਾ ਪ੍ਰਦੇਸ਼)

income_thresholds.description: Annual family income must be below Rs 5 lakh; white ration card holders are automatically eligible
income_thresholds.source: AP YSR Aarogyasri Guidelines / AP Government GO

exclusion_rules.description: Any family member paying income tax disqualifies the household
Only white ration card holders are eligible; families without white card excluded
Government employees with separate health coverage are excluded
Families already covered by employer health insurance are excluded
Families owning more than 35 acres of agricultural land (wet or dry) are excluded from Aarogyasri
Families owning more than 1 motorized vehicle (four-wheeler) are excluded as an asset restriction

family_criteria.family_definition: All members listed on the white ration card are covered. No cap on family size. Coverage is floater across all listed members.

covered_procedures: 3255 listed medical therapies and surgical procedures
Cardiology (bypass surgery, angioplasty, valve replacement, pacemaker)
Orthopedic (knee replacement, hip replacement, spinal surgery, fracture fixation)
Oncology (chemotherapy, radiation therapy, surgical oncology, bone marrow transplant)
Neurosurgery (brain tumor removal, craniotomy, spinal cord surgery)
Liver transplant (up to Rs 10 lakh additional coverage)
Heart transplant (up to Rs 10 lakh additional coverage)
Bone marrow transplant (up to Rs 10 lakh additional coverage)
Diagnostics and lab tests during hospitalization
Auto PM-JAY integration for eligible beneficiaries

required_documents.name: Aadhaar Card
required_documents.notes: Required for biometric/OTP-based e-KYC at empanelled hospital

required_documents.name: White Ration Card (Andhra Pradesh)
alternatives: BPL Certificate issued by AP Revenue Department
required_documents.notes: Must be a valid white ration card issued by AP Civil Supplies; primary eligibility document. Asset restrictions apply.

required_documents.name: Aarogyasri Health Card
required_documents.notes: Legacy card; now Aadhaar-based e-KYC is the primary verification method

required_documents.name: Mobile Number
required_documents.notes: For OTP verification and scheme communication

required_documents.name: Address Proof
required_documents.notes: AP residency verification; white ration card usually serves dual purpose

enrollment_channels: YSR Aarogyasri empanelled hospital help desk
enrollment_steps: Step 1: Check eligibility via 104 helpline or visit village/ward Sachivalayam with white ration card
Step 2: Visit YSR Aarogyasri empanelled hospital or district office with Aadhaar and white ration card
Step 3: Complete Aadhaar-based e-KYC (biometric or OTP verification)
Step 4: Hospital Aarogyasri coordinator verifies ration card and asset details against AP Civil Supplies database
Step 5: Pre-authorization request raised by hospital for the required medical procedure
Step 6: Aarogyasri Trust reviews and approves pre-authorization (emergency cases fast-tracked)
Step 7: Cashless treatment at empanelled hospital; auto-integrated with PM-JAY for dual coverage
description_for_embedding: Dr. YSR Aarogyasri is Andhra Pradesh's comprehensive state health insurance scheme providing Rs 5 lakh (Rs 500000) per family per year for secondary and tertiary hospitalization. Additional coverage of Rs 10 lakh is available for organ transplants including liver, heart, and bone marrow transplants. The scheme covers families holding white ration cards with annual income below Rs 5 lakh. Over 3255 medical therapies and surgical procedures are covered across all major specialties at empanelled public and private hospitals across AP. Asset restrictions apply: families owning more than 35 acres of land or more than 1 motorized vehicle are excluded. The scheme has automatic PM-JAY integration for eligible beneficiaries. Originally launched as Rajiv Aarogyasri in 2007, it was one of India's first state health insurance schemes. Free cashless treatment for all eligible families. Portal: ysraarogyasri.ap.gov.in.
